completed_tasks.name: Generic comparisons and archived anchor locks
Fresh v1.6 proof bundle generation and paper-grade report hardening
completed_tasks.progress: Fresh `v1.6-standard` and `v1.6-showcase` campaigns were rerun and inspected. Multiple proof-bundle dry runs exposed report-layer issues in `src/eml_symbolic_regression/campaign.py`, which are now fixed and covered by `tests/test_campaign.py` (17 passed). An isolated rerun of `campaign proof-shallow-pure-blind` completed successfully, and its `report.md` now matches `aggregate.json`: 3/18 verifier recoveries, including 2 threshold-eligible pure blind recoveries plus 1 repaired candidate.

remaining_tasks.name: Run the full authoritative v1.6 proof bundle from the final code state
Verify regenerated `proof-shallow`, `proof-basin`, and top-level `proof-report.md` against aggregate counts and threshold rows
Only after the proof bundle is authoritative, decide whether to archive/tag v1.6

blockers.description: `artifacts/proof/v1.6` contains mixed provenance from one completed dry run and multiple aborted reruns. The isolated `proof-shallow-pure-blind` suite is current, but the top-level proof bundle and the other proof-suite reports are not yet authoritative from the latest code state.
blockers.workaround: Rerun `PYTHONPATH=src python -m eml_symbolic_regression.cli proof-campaign --output-root artifacts/proof/v1.6 --overwrite`, then verify rewritten report mtimes and contents before citing any `artifacts/proof/v1.6` top-level artifact.

decisions.decision: Treat the fresh v1.6 paper bundle as a post-phase follow-up rather than silently trusting the first proof-campaign run.
decisions.rationale: The first completed proof bundle exposed report inaccuracies that did not affect core aggregates but would weaken publication-grade reporting if left uncorrected.

decisions.decision: Make `campaign.py` report generation regime-aware and evidence-class-aware before trusting any fresh proof output.
decisions.rationale: Dry-run inspection showed three real failures: blind-only proof reports mentioning warm-start evidence, perturbed-tree same-AST returns being dropped from campaign-level counts, and pure-blind report prose overstating threshold-eligible recoveries by counting repaired candidates.

decisions.decision: Use an isolated `proof-shallow-pure-blind` rerun as the gating canary before attempting another full proof-campaign rerun.
decisions.rationale: That suite exposed every report-layer issue fastest and now provides a verified reference point for the final full rerun.

next_action: Start with `PYTHONPATH=src python -m eml_symbolic_regression.cli proof-campaign --output-root artifacts/proof/v1.6 --overwrite`, then verify the rewritten `proof-shallow/report.md`, `proof-basin/report.md`, and `proof-report.md` against their aggregates before citing any v1.6 proof artifact.
context_notes: Safe pause state: no proof-campaign or pytest processes are running. The `proof-shallow-pure-blind` campaign is freshly regenerated from the latest code state and should be used as the correctness reference. The next session should not trust file counts while overwriting `artifacts/proof/v1.6`; use report mtimes and content checks instead. Expected during proof runs: repeated `RuntimeWarning: overflow encountered in exp` from `src/eml_symbolic_regression/semantics.py:208` on stress-path blind cases.
